name: hypothesis-generation
tagline: Builds literature-grounded hypotheses, experiments, and a LaTeX report; mandates calling an image-gen skill for figures.
verdict: No secret theft, destructive ops, or hidden instructions found. But allowed-tools omits WebFetch/WebSearch the workflow actually uses (H07); real scope (web/PubMed fetches, third-party image-gen API calls, LaTeX toolchain) exceeds the short description (S11); 'MANDATORY... not optional' figure step pressures extra network calls/cost (S06); no fallback guidan

highlights.summary: Workflow directs WebFetch/WebSearch against PubMed/general web (lines 82-88) and a mandatory Bash call to a third-party AI image-gen API (lines 40-48); functional for research but not flagged in the s
"⚠️ MANDATORY... This is not optional" (lines 28-30) pressures the executing agent to always invoke a separate image-generation skill/API regardless of what the user actually asked for.

Frontmatter description promises only hypothesis formulation/predictions/mechanisms/experiments (line 3), but the workflow mandates web literature search, a full LaTeX report pipeline (lines 223-234),
